title: Manager creates employee
statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

title: Manager updates employee
statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

title: Hr-Admin deletes employee
statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a employee through the REST API. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

title: Manager views employee
statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

title: Submit leave request
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 2
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 3
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 4
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 5
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Approve leave request
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin.

title: Approve leave request — variant 2
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Approve leave request — variant 3
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Approve leave request — variant 4
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Approve leave request — variant 5
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Submit timesheet
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 2
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 3
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 4
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 5
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Run payroll
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 2
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 3
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 4
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 5
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Upload onboarding document
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected.

title: Upload onboarding document — variant 2
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Upload onboarding document — variant 3
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Upload onboarding document — variant 4
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Upload onboarding document — variant 5
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Export employee directory
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory.

title: Export employee directory — variant 2
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Export employee directory — variant 3
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Export employee directory — variant 4

title: Export employee directory — variant 5

title: Announcement rollout
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 2
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 3
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 4
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 5
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Schedule performance review
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 2
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 3
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 4
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 5
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Bulk import employees
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 2
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 3
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 4
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 5
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Reset employee password
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected.

title: Reset employee password — variant 2
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Reset employee password — variant 3
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Reset employee password — variant 4
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Reset employee password — variant 5
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: List employees pagination
statement: The employee list shall be paginated at 50 per page with cursor-based pagination.

title: Timezone handling
statement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC; UI shall render in user's configured timezone.
error_pathways: default to UTC when tz missing

title: Feature flag rollout
statement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment; toggles shall be audit-logged.

title: Webhook delivery
statement: Outbound webhooks shall retry on 5xx with exponential backoff up to 5 times; permanent failures shall be DLQ'd.

title: Session management
statement: A user session shall expire after 30 minutes of inactivity; expired sessions shall redirect to login.

title: Accessibility compliance
statement: All UI screens shall be keyboard-navigable and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios.
